perturbation.original_text: Floating Rate Payer Period 01 January, 01 February, 01 March, 01 April, End Dates: 01 May, 01 June, 01 July, 01 August, 01 September, 01 October, 01 November and 01 December, in each year, from and including 01 May 2006, up to and including the Termination Date, subject to adjustment in accordance with the Business DayConvention specified immediately below, and thereshall be No Adjustment to the Period End Dates.
perturbation.changed_text: Floating Rate Payer Period 01 January, 01 February, 01 March, 01 April, End Dates: 01 May, 01 June, 01 July, 01 August, 01 September, 01 October, 01 November and 01 December, in each year, from and including 01 May 2006, up to and including the Termination Date, subject to adjustment in accordance with the Business Day Convention specified immediately below, and there shall be Automatic Adjustment to the Period End Dates to align with standard Market Practices.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity by contradicting the original stipulation of 'No Adjustment to the Period End Dates' by now stating that there will be 'Automatic Adjustment to the Period End Dates'. It contradicts itself with the fixed end dates mentioned earlier.
perturbation.contradicted_text: General Terms Trade Date: 27 April 2006 Effective Date 01 April 2006 Termination Date: 1 April 2009 Calculation Amount: The lesser of (a) USD 20,000,000.00 and (b) the aggregate Principal Balance (as defined in the Pooling and Servicing Agreement) of the Class I-A-22 Certificates as of the last day of the relevant Calculation Period. Seller of the Cap: UBS AG Buyer of the Cap: Counterparty Calculation Agent: UBS AG Business Days: New York Broker: None Fixed Amounts Fixed Rate Payer: Counterparty Fixed Amount: USD[ ] Fixed Rate Payer Payment Date: 27 April 2006 Business Day Convention: Not Applicable Floating Amounts Floating Rate Payer: UBS AG Cap Rate: 5.0 percent per annum Floating Amount: To be determined in accordance with the following formula: Greater of (1)Calculation Amount * Floating Rate Day Count Fraction * (Floating Rate Option - Cap Rate) and (2) 0 Floating Rate Option: USD-LIBOR-BBA Designated Maturity: One Month Spread: None Floating Rate Day Count 30/360 Fraction: Floating Rate Payer Period 01 January, 01 February, 01 March, 01 April, End Dates: 01 May, 01 June, 01 July, 01 August, 01 September, 01 October, 01 November and 01 December, in each year, from and including 01 May 2006, up to and including the Termination Date, subject to adjustment in accordance with the Business Day Convention specified immediately below, and there shall be Automatic Adjustment to the Period End Dates to align with standard Market Practices.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated 'No Adjustment to the Period End Dates.' The changed text introduces 'Automatic Adjustment to the Period End Dates to align with standard Market Practices.' This directly contradicts the previous statement within the same paragraph, creating a clear in-text contradiction as per the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition. The contradiction arises between the fixed end dates mentioned and the allowance for automatic adjustments.

perturbation.original_text: Additional Termination Events: The following Additional Termination Events will apply to UBS AG: 1. Ratings Event. If a Ratings Event (as defined below) has occurred and UBS AG has not complied with the requirements set forth in the succeeding paragraph within the 30 day time period specified therein, then an Additional Termination Event shall have occurred with respect to UBS AG and UBS AG shall be the sole Affected Party with respect to such an Additional Termination Event.
perturbation.changed_text: Additional Termination Events: The following Additional Termination Events will apply to UBS AG: 1. Ratings Event. If a Ratings Event (as defined below) has occurred and UBS AG has not complied with the requirements set forth in the succeeding paragraph within the 30 day time period specified therein, then an Additional Termination Event may occur with respect to UBS AG and Counterparty will decide on best course of action.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall have occurred' to 'may occur' and giving Counterparty discretion, the contract contradicts itself on whether a rating event automatically triggers termination. This creates an in-text contradiction with the certainty implied by the original wording.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Additional Termination Events: The following Additional Termination Events will apply to UBS AG: 1. Ratings Event. If a Ratings Event (as defined below) has occurred and UBS AG has not complied with the requirements set forth in the succeeding paragraph within the 30 day time period specified therein, then an Additional Termination Event may occur with respect to UBS AG and Counterparty will decide on best course of action. Rating Agency Downgrade: If a Ratings Event occurs with respect to UBS AG, then UBS AG shall, at its own expense, (i) assign this Transaction hereunder to a third party within thirty (30) days of such Ratings Event that meets or exceeds, or as to which any applicable credit support provider meets or exceeds, the Approved Rating Threshold (as defined below) on terms substantially similar to this Confirmation or (ii) deliver collateral acceptable in a form and amount acceptable to Fitch Ratings ("Fitch") and Moody's Investors Service Inc. ("Moody's) within thirty (30) days of such Ratings Event and subject to written confirmation from Fitch and Moody's that delivery of such collateral in the context of such downgrade will not result in a withdrawal, qualification or downgrade of the then current ratings assigned to the Certificates.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that if a Ratings Event occurs and UBS AG does not comply, then an Additional Termination Event 'shall have occurred'. The changed text modifies this to 'may occur' and grants Counterparty the discretion to decide on the best course of action. This introduces a direct contradiction by replacing a mandatory outcome with a discretionary one, conflicting with the original certainty and creating ambiguity. This also goes against the first Perturbation Definition: Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction which occurs when 'key terms or obligations are defined inconsistently in different parts of a contract, making enforcement difficult or interpretation unclear.'

perturbation.original_text: No transfer, amendment, waiver, supplement, assignment or other modification of this Transaction (other than the pledge of this Transaction to the Master Servicer pursuant to the Pooling and Servicing Agreement) shall be permitted by either party unless Moody's and Fitch have been provided notice of the same and confirm in writing (including by facsimile transmission) that they will not downgrade, qualify, withdraw or otherwise modify its then-current rating of the Certificates; provided however that except with respect to a transfer at the direction of UBS, nothing in this provision shall impose any obligation on UBS to give notice to any rating agency.
perturbation.changed_text: No transfer, amendment, waiver, supplement, assignment or other modification of this Transaction (other than the pledge of this Transaction to the Master Servicer pursuant to the Pooling and Servicing Agreement) may be permitted by either party unless they deem it appropriate, without any rating agency approval requirements.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by removing the explicit requirement for rating agency approval. This contradicts the implied agreement to seek approval from Moody's and Fitch when making modifications to the transaction, creating uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Transfer, Amendment and Assignment: No transfer, amendment, waiver, supplement, assignment or other modification of this Transaction (other than the pledge of this Transaction to the Master Servicer pursuant to the Pooling and Servicing Agreement) may be permitted by either party unless they deem it appropriate, without any rating agency approval requirements. Permitted Security Interest: For purposes of Section 7 of the Agreement, UBS AG hereby consents to the Permitted Security Interest. "Permitted Security Interest" means the collateral assignment by the Counterparty of the Cap Collateral to the Master Servicer pursuant to the Pooling and Servicing Agreement, and the granting to the Master Servicer of a security interest in the Cap Collateral pursuant to the Pooling and Servicing Agreement. "Cap Collateral" means all right, title and interest of the Counterparty in this Agreement, each Transaction hereunder, and all present and future amounts payable by UBS AG to the Counterparty under or in connection with the Agreement or any Transaction governed by the Agreement, whether or not evidenced by a Confirmation, including, without limitation, any transfer or termination of any such Transaction.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires rating agency approval for modifications to the transaction, while the changed text explicitly removes this requirement, stating modifications may be permitted without any rating agency approval. This directly contradicts the initial implied obligation to seek approval, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'. The change removes essential language that creates a conflict with the original intention.
